title: Blanket Channels: Choosing What the Agent Sees

runtime.expected_outcome: Turning on/off channels on the SharedContracts.Blanket changes the A matrix used by BundleBuilder and the observable posterior entropy.

symbols.A matrix: observation model over active blanket channels
symbols.H: posterior entropy; monotonic in channel subset

audiences.kid: Cover one of your senses and you can't tell apart things that used to be easy. More senses, fewer mistakes.
audiences.real: The Markov blanket declares which observation channels flow to the agent. Subtracting a channel shrinks the A matrix; the posterior gets fuzzier.
audiences.equation: H[q(s|o)] is monotonic non-decreasing as we subset the channels (data processing inequality).
audiences.derivation: By the data processing inequality, I[s; full o] >= I[s; subset]. Since H[s|full] = H[s] - I[s; full], subsetting cannot decrease conditional entropy (Cover & Thomas 2006).

orchestrate.objective: Show channel-subsetting's effect on posterior entropy using the Blanket abstraction.
orchestrate.role: Blanket-invariant tutor; clarifies the Markov blanket contract.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns; toggle {:goal_cue, :wall_*} channels on/off via a custom Blanket config.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 2.
Cover & Thomas (2006), Elements of Information Theory.
